message:
style(web): CSS updates for motion policy and component refinements

Comprehensive CSS updates implementing the motion policy and improving
component styling:

variables.css:
- Added duration tokens: --duration-instant (50ms) through
  --duration-slower (600ms)
- Added easing tokens: --ease-linear through --ease-expo-out
- New spring easings: --ease-spring, --ease-spring-soft
- Touch-device-aware timing adjustments

components.css:
- Overlay animation choreography implementation
- Staggered content reveal (60ms delay between children)
- Backdrop blur with fade (250ms, ease-out)
- Panel slide-up with spring physics (400ms, 80ms delay)
- Faster exit animations (180ms) for responsiveness
- Consistent modal/overlay styling

action-drawer.css:
- Updated for ACTION_DRAWER_SECTIONS structure
- Section headers and dividers
- Better touch target sizing for mobile
- Improved spacing and typography

control-deck.css:
- Refined control deck layout
- Better button grouping
- Touch-friendly sizing on mobile

index.css:
- Updated global reset/normalization
- Better focus-visible styling
- Improved scrollbar styling

mobile-overrides.css:
- Touch device specific overrides
- Larger touch targets
- Disabled hover effects on touch

mobile-ux.css:
- Bottom sheet animation improvements
- Swipe gesture indicator styling
- Safe area inset handling

typography.css:
- Added font smoothing improvements
- Better monospace font stack
- Improved line height for readability

styles.css:
- Root-level motion policy enforcement
- prefers-reduced-motion media query block
- Coarse pointer media query for touch devices
- Heavy FX suppression rules

All changes follow the motion policy documented in docs/motion-policy.md:
- Respect prefers-reduced-motion
- Disable continuous animations on touch devices
- Use semantic duration/easing tokens

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
